text: Git branching is a powerful feature in version control that helps teams work on different tasks simultaneously. When you create a feature branch, you make a separate copy of the code to work on a new feature without affecting the main project. Once your work is complete, you can submit a pull request, which allows team members to review your changes before they are added. After approval, you merge the branch back into the main codebase. This workflow keeps the main branch stable and prevents conflicts, making collaboration smoother and more organized.
